channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Russia ‘dangerously’ intercepts British spy plane over Black Sea: Ministry
item.description: The incident came last month when unarmed Rivet Joint plane was securing NATO's eastern flank in international airspace.

item.title: Meta cuts 8,000 jobs in sweeping global layoffs
item.description: Meta cancels hiring plans for 6,000 roles amid ongoing layoffs and a pivot to artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Controlled reopening ends Iran’s lengthy stock market shutdown
item.description: Stocks in companies hit by US and Israeli strikes, such as energy and steel firms, didn't take part in the reopening.

item.title: Trump administration indicts Cuba’s Raul Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
item.description: The indictment marks one of the sharpest escalations in tensions between Washington and Havana.

item.title: Trump jokes about running for Israeli prime minister
item.description: Donald Trump joked that he could run for Israeli prime minister after claiming he enjoys ‘99%’ support in Israel.

item.title: Iran claims it coordinated passage of 26 vessels out of Hormuz in 24 hours
item.description: The IRGC claims to maintain control over the Strait of Hormuz despite the US blockade on Iran's ports.

item.title: At least eight killed in Israel’s air attacks on southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon continue despite the 'ceasefire' that was recently extended until the beginning of July.

item.title: Oscar-winning director calls Trump, Netanyahu and Putin ‘monsters’
item.description: Oscar-winning Spanish director Pedro Almodovar called Donald Trump, Benjamin Netanyahu and Vladimir Putin ‘monsters’.

item.title: The battle of perception: From Israel’s Fauda to Hezbollah’s FPV footage
item.description: New approaches to war propaganda are reshaping the conflict between Israel, Hezbollah, and Iran.

item.title: What is the Power of Siberia 2 pipeline that Russia, China are planning?
item.description: Analysts say Russia wants to replace lost gas revenue while China seeks to diversify and secure its energy supplies.

item.title: Families demand justice for victims killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
item.description: Two childhood neighbours say they are fighting for justice after an Israeli strike killed five of their relatives.

item.title: Why is Trump’s Gaza Board of Peace facing a funding shortfall?
item.description: Experts say lack of a political horizon and Israeli land grabs are deterring donors from funding Gaza's reconstruction.

item.title: Is the West de-risking from China or containing its economy?
item.description: As the US and Europe push to reduce reliance on China, Beijing tightens control over supply chains.

item.title: Massie defeated: The Israel lobby’s pyrrhic victory in Kentucky
item.description: The campaign to unseat Thomas Massie exposed a growing Republican backlash against pro-Israel political influence.

item.title: ‘Multipolar world’: What Xi and Putin announced after Beijing summit
item.description: The Russian and Chinese leaders agreed on bilateral cooperation during Putin's visit to China.

item.title: Will Trump kill the $14bn Taiwan arms deal?
item.description: Will Trump kill $14bn Taiwan arms deal?

item.title: Israel’s ambassador to Christians gives rose-coloured spiel
item.description: George Deek, Israel’s ‘ambassador to Christians’ released a statement on X, to Christians in the region and globally.

item.title: Israel’s Ben-Gvir publishes video taunting detained flotilla activists
item.description: Israel’s Ben-Gvir publishes video taunting detained flotilla activists

item.title: North Korean women win in South to reach Asian Champions League final
item.description: Naegohyang Women's FC record a historic 2-1 win over Suwon FC Women on a rare trip to South Korea.

item.title: New York City hotels avert labour strike threat before FIFA World Cup
item.description: Hotel operators avoid a 'very real threat' by signing a deal with 25,000 workers as the city hosts the 2026 tournament.

item.title: WHO says 600 suspected cases, 139 deaths in growing Ebola outbreak
item.description: Numbers are expected to rise, the WHO warns, as European officials emphasise the risk of a European outbreak is low.

item.title: Israeli lawmakers advance bill to dissolve parliament
item.description: Netanyahu is under mounting pressure as his fractious right-wing coalition submitted the bill to dissolve parliament.

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Gaza flotilla organisers: Why it matters
item.description: Nearly all flotillas over 20 years have been intercepted by Israel. Hundreds remain missing after latest interdiction.

item.title: Why Ebola keeps returning to DRC: A heartbreaking human toll
item.description: Families in Mongbwalu grieve as Ebola returns, exposing serious gaps in healthcare and outbreak preparedness.

item.title: Which teams will make their debut at World Cup 2026?
item.description: Cape Verde, Curacao, Jordan and Uzbekistan will take part in football's premier showpiece for the first time.
